diffs.diff:
--- a/debug_cameras2.py
+++ b/debug_cameras2.py
@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
+import sys

+sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')

+

+import inspect

+from tsr.utils import get_spherical_cameras

+

+print("=== get_spherical_cameras source ===")

+print(inspect.getsource(get_spherical_cameras))

+

+# Also check what it returns for a known pose

+import torch

+import math

+

+# Test with a single view at known azimuth/elevation

+rays_o, rays_d = get_spherical_cameras(

+    n_views=1,

+    elevation_deg=0.0,

+    camera_distance=1.9,

+    fovy_deg=40.0,

+    height=256,

+    width=256

+)

+print(f"\nrays_o shape: {rays_o.shape}")

+print(f"rays_d shape: {rays_d.shape}")

+print(f"rays_o[0,0,0]: {rays_o[0,0,0]}")

+print(f"rays_o[0,128,128]: {rays_o[0,128,128]}")

+print(f"rays_d[0,0,0]: {rays_d[0,0,0]}")

+print(f"rays_d[0,128,128]: {rays_d[0,128,128]}")

+

+# Test with 4 views to see azimuth pattern

+rays_o4, rays_d4 = get_spherical_cameras(

+    n_views=4,

+    elevation_deg=0.0,

+    camera_distance=1.9,

+    fovy_deg=40.0,

+    height=256,

+    width=256

+)

+print(f"\n4 views rays_o shape: {rays_o4.shape}")

+for i in range(4):

+    print(f"  View {i} camera pos: {rays_o4[i,128,128]}")

+

+# Now test my custom function

+print("\n=== My custom function ===")

+import torch.nn.functional as F

+

+def get_camera_rays(azimuth_deg, elevation_deg, camera_distance, height=256, width=256, fovy_deg=40.0):

+    elevation = math.radians(elevation_deg)

+    azimuth = math.radians(azimuth_deg)

+    

+    camera_position = torch.tensor([

+        camera_distance * math.cos(elevation) * math.cos(azimuth),

+        camera_distance * math.cos(elevation) * math.sin(azimuth),

+        camera_distance * math.sin(elevation),

+    ], dtype=torch.float32)

+    

+    center = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float32)

+    up = torch.tensor([0.0, 0.0, 1.0], dtype=torch.float32)

+    

+    lookat = F.normalize(center - camera_position, dim=-1)

+    right = F.normalize(torch.cross(lookat, up, dim=-1), dim=-1)

+    up = F.normalize(torch.cross(right, lookat, dim=-1), dim=-1)

+    

+    c2w = torch.zeros(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32)

+    c2w[:3, 0] = right

+    c2w[:3, 1] = up

+    c2w[:3, 2] = -lookat

+    c2w[:3, 3] = camera_position

+    c2w[3, 3] = 1.0

+    

+    fovy = math.radians(fovy_deg)

+    focal_length = 0.5 * height / math.tan(0.5 * fovy)

+    

+    x = torch.arange(width, dtype=torch.float32) + 0.5

+    y = torch.arange(height, dtype=torch.float32) + 0.5

+    yy, xx = torch.meshgrid(y, x, indexing='ij')

+    

+    directions = torch.stack(

+        [(xx - width / 2.0) / focal_length, 

+         (yy - height / 2.0) / focal_length, 

+         -torch.ones_like(xx)], dim=-1

+    )

+    

+    rays_d = torch.sum(directions[..., None, :] * c2w[:3, :3], dim=-1)

+    rays_o = c2w[:3, 3].expand(rays_d.shape)

+    rays_d = F.normalize(rays_d, dim=-1)

+    

+    return rays_o.unsqueeze(0), rays_d.unsqueeze(0)

+

+# Compare at azimuth=0, elevation=0

+my_o, my_d = get_camera_rays(0.0, 0.0, 1.9)

+print(f"My rays_o[0,128,128]: {my_o[0,128,128]}")

+print(f"My rays_d[0,128,128]: {my_d[0,128,128]}")

+print(f"TripoSR rays_o[0,128,128]: {rays_o[0,128,128]}")

+print(f"TripoSR rays_d[0,128,128]: {rays_d[0,128,128]}")

+

+# Compare at azimuth=90, elevation=0

+rays_o90, rays_d90 = get_spherical_cameras(

+    n_views=4,

+    elevation_deg=0.0,

+    camera_distance=1.9,

+    fovy_deg=40.0,

+    height=256,

+    width=256

+)

+my_o90, my_d90 = get_camera_rays(90.0, 0.0, 1.9)

+print(f"\nAt az=90:")

+print(f"My camera pos: {my_o90[0,128,128]}")

+print(f"TripoSR view 1 (az=90) pos: {rays_o90[1,128,128]}")

+

+# Check what azimuth convention TripoSR uses

+print(f"\nTripoSR 4-view camera positions:")

+for i in range(4):

+    pos = rays_o4[i,128,128]

+    az = math.degrees(math.atan2(pos[1].item(), pos[0].item()))

+    el = math.degrees(math.asin(pos[2].item() / torch.norm(pos).item()))

+    print(f"  View {i}: pos={pos.tolist()}, computed az={az:.1f}, el={el:.1f}")
